zh.Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Deuts: 德國爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀
zh.lifeSummary: 當Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige於大約1155年出生在德國爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀時，他的父親Egino III. Graf von Urach32歲，母親Kunigunde von Wasserburg, Gräfin im Swiggerstal17歲。他於1180年與Agnes von Zähringen在德國爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1218年被列為regentschaft in freiburg。他去世於1237年7月25日在德國巴登-爾特波格可萊斯 埃門丁根福亞姆特騰能巴賀享年83歲，埋葬在德國巴登-爾特波格可萊斯 埃門丁根福亞姆特騰能巴賀。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige于大约1155年出生在德国爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀时，他的父亲Egino III. Graf von Urach32岁，母亲Kunigunde von Wasserburg, Gräfin im Swiggerstal17岁。他于1180年与Agnes von Zähringen在德国爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1218年被列为regentschaft in freiburg。他去世于1237年7月25日在德国巴登-爾特波格可萊斯 埃門丁根福亞姆特騰能巴賀享年83岁，埋葬在德国巴登-爾特波格可萊斯 埃門丁根福亞姆特騰能巴賀。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Urach
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ulrich  .
English: probably from the Old English personal name Wulfrīc (see Wooldridge ).

Scottish: shortened form of McUrich, itself an Anglicized form (with loss of the internal fricative) of Gaelic Mac Íomhair ‘son of Íomhar’, Gaelic form of the Old Norse personal name Ívarr (see Iverson 2).

variants.name: Ulrich
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Hans, Gerhard, Klaus, Frieda, Fritz, Reinhard, Alfons, Alois, Bernd.

German: from the personal name Ulrich, Old High German Odalrīc, composed of the elements odal ‘inherited property, fortune’ + rīc ‘power’. The name was borne by a 10th-century Christian saint, bishop of Augsburg, whose fame contributed greatly to the popularity of the personal name in German- and Slavic-speaking areas in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Urish
meanings: Probably an altered form of German   Urich  .

variants.name: Urick
meanings: Americanized form of German   Urich  .
Americanized form of Slovak and Czech Urík or Urik: unexplained.
Americanized form of Slovak   Jurik   and also of Polish   Jurek  .

nameMeanings.name: Egino
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the many given names ending in -gino, e.g. Giorgino, pet form of Giorgio , and Luigino, pet form of Luigi .

nameMeanings.name: IV.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige was born about 1155, in Urach, Urach, Württemberg, Germany, his father, Egino III. Graf von Urach, was 32 and his mother, Kunigunde von Wasserburg, Gräfin im Swiggerstal, was 17. He married Agnes von Zähringen in 1180, in Urach, Urach, Württemberg, Germany. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. In 1218, his occupation is listed as regentschaft in freiburg. He died on 25 July 1237, in Tennenbach, Freiamt, Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Germany, at the age of 83, and was buried in Tennenbach, Freiamt, Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige est né environ 1155, à Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Allemagne, son père, Egino III. Graf von Urach, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Kunigunde von Wasserburg, Gräfin im Swiggerstal, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Agnes von Zähringen en 1180, à Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. En 1218, sa profession de son indiquée est regentschaft in freiburg. Il est décédé le 25 juillet 1237, à Tennenbach, Freiamt, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterré à Tennenbach, Freiamt, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige um 1155 in Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Egino III. Graf von Urach 32 und seine Mutter Kunigunde von Wasserburg, Gräfin im Swiggerstal 17. Er hat 1180 in Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Deutschland Agnes von Zähringen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. 1218 ist sein Beruf als regentschaft in freiburg aufgeführt. Er starb am 25. Juli 1237 im Alter von 83 Jahren in Tennenbach, Freiamt, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland und wurde in Tennenbach, Freiamt, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige è nato il circa 1155 nel Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Germania, il suo padre, Egino III. Graf von Urach, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Kunigunde von Wasserburg, Gräfin im Swiggerstal, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Agnes von Zähringen nel 1180, nel Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Nel 1218, la sua occupazione è indicata come regentschaft in freiburg. Morì il 25 luglio 1237, nel Tennenbach, Freiamt, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Germania, all'età di 83 anni, e fu sepolto a Tennenbach, Freiamt, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Germania.

ja.Tennenbach, Freiamt, Emmendingen, Baden-: ドイツバデン-ウuルッテンバーグクレイス エンメンディンジェンフレイアムトテネンバチ
ja.Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Deuts: ドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト ウラチウラチ
ja.lifeSummary: Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtigeが約1155年にドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト ウラチウラチで生まれた時，父Egino III. Graf von Urachは32歳，母Kunigunde von Wasserburg, Gräfin im Swiggerstalは17歳であった。1180年にドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト ウラチウラチでAgnes von Zähringenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。1218年，職業はregentschaft in freiburgと記録されている。彼は1237年7月25日にドイツバデン-ウuルッテンバーグクレイス エンメンディンジェンフレイアムトテネンバチで83歳で死去し，ドイツバデン-ウuルッテンバーグクレイス エンメンディンジェンフレイアムトテネンバチにて埋葬された。

ko.Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendin: 독일 바든 크레이스 에멘딩 에멘딩 에멘딩
ko.Tennenbach, Freiamt, Emmendingen, Baden-: 독일 바데-wu르테버그 크레이스 에멘딩 프레이아트 텐바치
독일 바데-wu르테버그 크레이스 에멘딩 프레이아트 텐바치
ko.lifeSummary: Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige(이)가 대략 1155년 독일 wu르테버그 오베램트 라치 라치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Egino III. Graf von Urach(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Kunigunde von Wasserburg, Gräfin im Swiggerstal(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1180년 독일 wu르테버그 오베램트 라치 라치에서 Agnes von Zähringen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1218년, 그의 직업은 regentschaft in freiburg이었다. 그는 1237년 7월 25일 독일 바데-wu르테버그 크레이스 에멘딩 프레이아트 텐바치에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 바데-wu르테버그 크레이스 에멘딩 프레이아트 텐바치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige nasceu aproximadamente 1155, em Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Alemanha, seu pai, Egino III. Graf von Urach, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Kunigunde von Wasserburg, Gräfin im Swiggerstal, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes von Zähringen em 1180, em Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Em 1218, sua profissão aparece como regentschaft in freiburg. Ele faleceu em 25 de julho de 1237, em Tennenbach, Freiamt, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Alemanha, com 83 anos, e foi sepultado em Tennenbach, Freiamt, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Alemanha.

тенненбач, фриамт, крис эмменденген, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige родился около 1155 г. в местности урэич, обирамт урэич, вuрттембирг, Германия, его отцу по имени Egino III. Graf von Urach было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Kunigunde von Wasserburg, Gräfin im Swiggerstal, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes von Zähringen в 1180 г. в местности урэич, обирамт урэич, вuрттембирг, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. В 1218 году он работал по профессии regentschaft in freiburg. Он умер 25 июля 1237 г. в местности тенненбач, фриамт, крис эмменденген, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия в возрасте 83 лет и был похоронен в местности тенненбач, фриамт, крис эмменденген, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige nació aproximadamente 1155, en Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Alemania, su papá, Egino III. Graf von Urach, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Kunigunde von Wasserburg, Gräfin im Swiggerstal, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Agnes von Zähringen en 1180, en Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. En 1218, su ocupación registrada era regentschaft in freiburg. Murió el 25 de julio de 1237, en Tennenbach, Freiamt, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Alemania, a la edad de 83 años, y fue sepultado en Tennenbach, Freiamt, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Alemania.
